mentions.note: Up 84% in 2025 under new CEO Lip Bhutan; Trump admin $9B injection, Nvidia $5B follow-up months later; balance sheet improving. Multi-year turnaround ahead but directional shift positive. Cramer confident on trajectory.
Hardware powering AI soaring; once-lowly worm now benefiting from AI infrastructure build.

Most surprising 2025 comeback; CEO Pat Gelsinger secured ~$9B federal bailout and $5B Nvidia investment. Textbook uptrend, MACD bullish, OBV climbing; RSI overbought but can sustain weeks. Running at $55, could reach 2021 highs $60s–70s. Federal + Nvidia validation massive revaluation driver.
Memory chip plays mentioned; Intel not explicitly discussed but memory makers rallying may indicate shift in semiconductor hierarchy.
Stock flying since Lip Bhutan became CEO; reclaiming America's semiconductor mantle, but after recent run, actual earnings may not be big enough to justify valuation—give this one a rest.
Up 40% YTD despite weak quarter; demand for chips insane; CEO Pat Gelsinger has more up his sleeve per Cramer; do not give up on this stock—mistake to abandon it now.

Down 17% on Q1 sub-optimal guidance due to inability to produce CPUs fast enough despite massive shortage; execution miss not demand problem. Still great company under Lip Buu; will take advantage of CPU shortage.
Missed guidance on CPU shortage (supply constraint, not demand); down 17% but still great company under Lip Boutan. Will benefit from CPU shortage despite poor execution this quarter.
Reported good numbers but dismal guidance on CPU supply constraints; lacks manufacturing equipment; doesn't have ability to meet chip demand; signals more business for capital equipment providers.

#10 January performer, up ~26% despite Q4 miss. Supply constraints (not demand weakness) driving weak Q1 guidance. Stock more than doubled since US govt $9B 10% stake Aug. CEO Lip Boutan credible; more upside ahead.
Buyback of Irish facility (sold to PE when cash-strapped) signals balance sheet strength recovery; rock-solid balance sheet now after teetering.
Hardware renaissance under CEO Lip-Bu Tan; back from dead as AI capex boom drives chip demand.
CPU short supply tailwind; stocks up significantly but fundamentally sound alternative to speculative compute infrastructure pivots.
Thursday evening earnings; remarkable turnaround under Pat Gelsinger on CPU and high-end packaging; street graded poorly last time; do not buy until quarter released, then piece them up.
Soared on CPU demand for AI; beneficiary of next-generation compute needs; critical infrastructure play.

Up 59% since March 30 (top performer). New CEO Lip-Bu Tan stabilizing business; US government and Nvidia both took stakes last year (now up 2–3x). CPUs are new AI bottleneck. Q1 earnings Thursday night; stock moved fast, analysts may have missed it.
CPU provider needed for agentic AI agents; buyers insatiable despite Nvidia dominance; Cramer regrets missing.
Reported magnificent surprise top and bottom line; CEO Lip Bu Tan delivered miracle quarter in 13 months tenure. Cannot overcome software sector stench, but solid execution.

Astonishing resurrection: beat Q1 expectations massively (+7% revenue, $0.29 EPS vs. $0.01 expected), guided strong Q2 (+7-10%, $0.20 EPS). Fourth industrial revolution (agentics) driving CPU demand; ratio shifting 1:8→1:4→1:1 (Intel vs. Nvidia). CEO Pat Gelsinger proving profound cultural shift. Stock quadrupled in 9 months; re-rating early innings. Buy on pullback—long-term story best in 25 years.
CPU complex will do well rest of year. Last week explosion of buying on Intel story; copycat sellers pancaked — real good chance to buy them on pullbacks.
CPU shortage crippling AI agent growth; climbed 12% as inability to alleviate shortage is exactly what market wants. Capacity wall bullish.
YoY +399% return; foundry turnaround and semis recovery; individual stock wealth creation opportunity.
Historical CPU glut has flipped to shortage driven by AI data center agent demand; same value-to-growth re-rating thesis applies as with AMD.
CPU provider previously seen as worthless now worth gold due to AI and data center demand; can't seem to stop it at all.
Semiconductor manufacturer in chips/semis AI infrastructure layer. U.S. chip manufacturing revival tied to AI buildout.
Massive gains riding data-center wave; CPU production essential for AI platform deployment; market loves.
Mentioned as cheaper valuation than Cerebras in context of AI chip race; no new fundamental news but valuation more reasonable than hot IPO.

Lip Bu Tan's remarkable turnaround: $20→$108 in 12 months. Culture flattening, federal support ($5B Nvidia, $2B SoftBank), simplified roadmap, AI-driven CPU demand, foundry yield recovery, advanced packaging, and 14A (1.4nm) on track for 2028–2029 parity with TSMC.
Semiconductor powering AI; CEO Lip Bu Tan championed for backing semiconductor startups and valuing IP; important player but Nvidia dominates.
Did not hold up in late-day trading; weakness despite data center opportunities and Lip Bu Tan's packaging division leadership creates concern.
CPU demand explosion from agentic AI as GPU-to-CPU ratio shifts from 8:1 toward 1:1; CEO Lip Bu Tan fixing foundry business; potential Apple and Musk orders on 18A node; own it, don't trade it.

Now my favorite stock in charitable trust; memory chip shortage severe but CPU shortage coming next and that's Intel's wheelhouse. Expecting Apple deal to materialize; club aggressively recommended buying.

Lip-Bu Tan flattened hierarchy to 5 layers, secured Google as foundry partner, and has Kora Rapid multi-threading product in pipeline; Cramer expects continued appreciation after the stock's run from 20 to 100+.
